mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth confirms corporate earnings expansion and reduces equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, labor market tightness signals sustained wage growth and consumption strength, boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE closes below 5.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and CPI indicate aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day forward returns in over 55% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise consecutively for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in an early-cycle expansion signals supply-side recovery and capacity utilization improvement, driving short-term equity gains as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises for three consecutive months but SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of those instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, leading to negative equity returns over the next month as housing-linked sectors drag on the index.
failure_condition: SPX remains positive over 20 days in more than 50% of cases where HOUST falls by more than 10% within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls confirms economic momentum, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in months with payroll growth exceed 150k are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 2.5%, a decline in retail sales indicates weakening demand amid price pressures, squeezing corporate margins and driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day forward returns in over 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above 2.5% and RSAFS declines for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: As the Fed raises rates above 0.5% into a normalization phase, higher discount rates and reduced liquidity pressure equity valuations, yielding negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% and continues to increase for three months.
